questions.stem: I ______ to Beijing three years ago. I ______ there twice since then.
questions.explanation: 第一空有明确过去时间状语three years ago，必须用一般过去时went；第二空有since then（从那以后），表示从过去持续到现在的经历，用现在完成时have been。have gone to表示去了某地（人未回），have been to表示去过某地（人已回），这里说去过两次，人在此地，所以用have been。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl ______ is singing on the stage is my classmate.
questions.explanation: 先行词是the girl（人），定语从句缺主语，用who引导。which用于先行词是物；whom用于指人但在从句中作宾语；whose表示所属关系。口诀：先行词是人作主语用who，作宾语用whom/who，先行词是物用which/that。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These books ______ to the library tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 书是被归还的对象，必须用被动语态。情态动词must后的被动结构是must be + 过去分词。return的过去分词是returned。A是主动语态；C缺少be；D中return应该用过去分词形式。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ______ be him. He has gone to Beijing for a meeting.
questions.explanation: 根据后句He has gone to Beijing（他去北京了，人不在），可确定敲门的不可能是他。表示否定推测'不可能'用can't。must表示肯定推测'一定是'；might表示不确定的推测'可能是'；should表示'应该'。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you are ______ with your current score, you should work harder to get a better one.
questions.explanation: be satisfied with是固定搭配，表示'对……感到满意'。satisfying令人满意的（修饰物）；satisfaction是名词；satisfy是动词。主语是人，表示人的感受，用-ed形容词satisfied。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The weather was terrible, so we had to ______ our picnic plan.
questions.explanation: 句意：天气很糟糕，所以我们不得不取消野餐计划。cancel（取消）符合语境。uncancel（恢复）前缀un-表示相反动作，但不符合句意；recancel（再次取消）前缀re-表示再次；discover（发现）前缀dis-加cover，与语境无关。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should take immediate actions to ______ the endangered animals.
questions.explanation: protect（保护）符合语境'保护濒危动物'。prevent（阻止）常与from搭配；prepare（准备）常与for搭配；pronounce（发音）。根据宾语endangered animals（濒危动物），应选择protect。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it would be too much trouble to change their habits. However, our teacher Mr. Li encouraged us by saying, "Small actions can make a big difference."

One of the first changes was to use reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. My classmate Tom, who always bought bottled water, decided to (8)______ his habit. He bought a nice metal bottle and filled it with water every morning. "It's not only good for the environment but also saves money," he said.

We also started a recycling program in our classroom. Every Friday afternoon, we would collect paper, bottles and other recyclable materials. At first, some students found it (9)______ to remember which bin to use. But after a few weeks, it became a natural part of our routine.

The most challenging part was saving electricity. Many of us were used to leaving the lights on when leaving the classroom. To solve this problem, we appointed "energy monitors" for each class. Their job was to (10)______ that all lights and computers were turned off after school.

After two months, the results were amazing. Our school's electricity bill dropped by 20%, and we recycled over 500 kilograms of waste. More importantly, students began to (11)______ that environmental protection wasn't just a slogan—it was something we could do every day.

This project has taught us that everyone can contribute to a better world. As the famous saying goes, "We do not inherit the earth from our ancestors; we borrow it from our children." I am proud to be part of this (12)______ change in our school.
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，前文提到之前总是买瓶装水，后文说买了金属水壶，说明是决定'改变'习惯。change（改变）符合语境。keep（保持）和follow（遵循）与后文矛盾；form（形成）通常指养成新习惯，这里指改掉旧习惯。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it would be too much trouble to change their habits. However, our teacher Mr. Li encouraged us by saying, "Small actions can make a big difference."

One of the first changes was to use reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. My classmate Tom, who always bought bottled water, decided to (8)______ his habit. He bought a nice metal bottle and filled it with water every morning. "It's not only good for the environment but also saves money," he said.

We also started a recycling program in our classroom. Every Friday afternoon, we would collect paper, bottles and other recyclable materials. At first, some students found it (9)______ to remember which bin to use. But after a few weeks, it became a natural part of our routine.

The most challenging part was saving electricity. Many of us were used to leaving the lights on when leaving the classroom. To solve this problem, we appointed "energy monitors" for each class. Their job was to (10)______ that all lights and computers were turned off after school.

After two months, the results were amazing. Our school's electricity bill dropped by 20%, and we recycled over 500 kilograms of waste. More importantly, students began to (11)______ that environmental protection wasn't just a slogan—it was something we could do every day.

This project has taught us that everyone can contribute to a better world. As the famous saying goes, "We do not inherit the earth from our ancestors; we borrow it from our children." I am proud to be part of this (12)______ change in our school.
questions.explanation: 根据后句But after a few weeks, it became natural（但几周后变得自然了），可推断起初觉得'困难'。difficult（困难的）与后文形成转折对比。easy（容易的）与后文矛盾；interesting（有趣的）和necessary（必要的）不符合转折逻辑。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it would be too much trouble to change their habits. However, our teacher Mr. Li encouraged us by saying, "Small actions can make a big difference."

One of the first changes was to use reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. My classmate Tom, who always bought bottled water, decided to (8)______ his habit. He bought a nice metal bottle and filled it with water every morning. "It's not only good for the environment but also saves money," he said.

We also started a recycling program in our classroom. Every Friday afternoon, we would collect paper, bottles and other recyclable materials. At first, some students found it (9)______ to remember which bin to use. But after a few weeks, it became a natural part of our routine.

The most challenging part was saving electricity. Many of us were used to leaving the lights on when leaving the classroom. To solve this problem, we appointed "energy monitors" for each class. Their job was to (10)______ that all lights and computers were turned off after school.

After two months, the results were amazing. Our school's electricity bill dropped by 20%, and we recycled over 500 kilograms of waste. More importantly, students began to (11)______ that environmental protection wasn't just a slogan—it was something we could do every day.

This project has taught us that everyone can contribute to a better world. As the famous saying goes, "We do not inherit the earth from our ancestors; we borrow it from our children." I am proud to be part of this (12)______ change in our school.
questions.explanation: 节能委员的职责应该是'检查'关灯情况。check（检查）符合语境。doubt（怀疑）、forget（忘记）、imagine（想象）都不符合节能委员的职责描述。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it would be too much trouble to change their habits. However, our teacher Mr. Li encouraged us by saying, "Small actions can make a big difference."

One of the first changes was to use reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. My classmate Tom, who always bought bottled water, decided to (8)______ his habit. He bought a nice metal bottle and filled it with water every morning. "It's not only good for the environment but also saves money," he said.

We also started a recycling program in our classroom. Every Friday afternoon, we would collect paper, bottles and other recyclable materials. At first, some students found it (9)______ to remember which bin to use. But after a few weeks, it became a natural part of our routine.

The most challenging part was saving electricity. Many of us were used to leaving the lights on when leaving the classroom. To solve this problem, we appointed "energy monitors" for each class. Their job was to (10)______ that all lights and computers were turned off after school.

After two months, the results were amazing. Our school's electricity bill dropped by 20%, and we recycled over 500 kilograms of waste. More importantly, students began to (11)______ that environmental protection wasn't just a slogan—it was something we could do every day.

This project has taught us that everyone can contribute to a better world. As the famous saying goes, "We do not inherit the earth from our ancestors; we borrow it from our children." I am proud to be part of this (12)______ change in our school.
questions.explanation: 根据句意'学生们开始意识到环保不只是口号'，realize（意识到）符合语境。refuse（拒绝）、promise（承诺）、regret（后悔）都不符合此处表达认知变化的语境。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started an environmental protection project called "Green Campus". The goal was to reduce waste and save energy in our daily school life.

At first, many students were not interested in it. They thought it would be too much trouble to change their habits. However, our teacher Mr. Li encouraged us by saying, "Small actions can make a big difference."

One of the first changes was to use reusable bottles instead of plastic ones. My classmate Tom, who always bought bottled water, decided to (8)______ his habit. He bought a nice metal bottle and filled it with water every morning. "It's not only good for the environment but also saves money," he said.

We also started a recycling program in our classroom. Every Friday afternoon, we would collect paper, bottles and other recyclable materials. At first, some students found it (9)______ to remember which bin to use. But after a few weeks, it became a natural part of our routine.

The most challenging part was saving electricity. Many of us were used to leaving the lights on when leaving the classroom. To solve this problem, we appointed "energy monitors" for each class. Their job was to (10)______ that all lights and computers were turned off after school.

After two months, the results were amazing. Our school's electricity bill dropped by 20%, and we recycled over 500 kilograms of waste. More importantly, students began to (11)______ that environmental protection wasn't just a slogan—it was something we could do every day.

This project has taught us that everyone can contribute to a better world. As the famous saying goes, "We do not inherit the earth from our ancestors; we borrow it from our children." I am proud to be part of this (12)______ change in our school.
questions.explanation: 根据全文积极的基调，以及前文提到的环保成果，meaningful（有意义的）符合语境。sudden（突然的）与after two months矛盾；strange（奇怪的）和meaningless（无意义的）与作者自豪的态度相反。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Many students sacrifice sleep to study for exams, thinking that more study time means better grades. However, recent scientific research shows that this might actually hurt academic performance.

Scientists at a university in the US conducted a study with 500 high school students. They found that students who slept less than 6 hours a night had trouble concentrating in class. Their test scores were 20% lower than those who slept 8 hours or more.

"Sleep is like food for the brain," explains Dr. Sarah Chen, a sleep expert. "During sleep, your brain processes and stores information you learned during the day. Without enough sleep, your brain cannot do this job well."

The study also found an interesting rhythm in learning efficiency. Students who took short naps (15-30 minutes) after lunch performed better in afternoon classes. This is because the brain needs rest to work at its best.

However, many teenagers struggle to get enough sleep. School schedules, homework pressure, and the use of electronic devices before bed all contribute to this problem. The blue light from phones and computers can strain your eyes and make it harder to fall asleep (strain: 使疲劳；损伤).

To solve this issue, experts suggest three things. First, establish a regular sleep schedule and stick to it even on weekends. Second, avoid using electronic devices one hour before bedtime. Third, create a comfortable sleep environment with proper temperature and darkness.

Remember, sacrificing sleep for study time is like borrowing money at high interest. You might gain an hour of study time, but you lose two hours of productivity the next day. Good sleep habits are not a waste of time—they are an investment in your academic success.
questions.stem: What did the scientists find in the study?
options: A. Students who slept less got higher scores.
B. More sleep always leads to better grades.
C. Students with less than 6 hours' sleep had lower test scores.
D. All students should sleep exactly 8 hours every night.
questions.explanation: 细节定位题。根据第二段'They found that students who slept less than 6 hours a night... Their test scores were 20% lower than those who slept 8 hours or more'，可知睡眠不足6小时的学生分数更低。A与原文相反；B中always过于绝对；D中exactly 8 hours不是研究结论，研究说的是8小时或以上。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Many students sacrifice sleep to study for exams, thinking that more study time means better grades. However, recent scientific research shows that this might actually hurt academic performance.

Scientists at a university in the US conducted a study with 500 high school students. They found that students who slept less than 6 hours a night had trouble concentrating in class. Their test scores were 20% lower than those who slept 8 hours or more.

"Sleep is like food for the brain," explains Dr. Sarah Chen, a sleep expert. "During sleep, your brain processes and stores information you learned during the day. Without enough sleep, your brain cannot do this job well."

The study also found an interesting rhythm in learning efficiency. Students who took short naps (15-30 minutes) after lunch performed better in afternoon classes. This is because the brain needs rest to work at its best.

However, many teenagers struggle to get enough sleep. School schedules, homework pressure, and the use of electronic devices before bed all contribute to this problem. The blue light from phones and computers can strain your eyes and make it harder to fall asleep (strain: 使疲劳；损伤).

To solve this issue, experts suggest three things. First, establish a regular sleep schedule and stick to it even on weekends. Second, avoid using electronic devices one hour before bedtime. Third, create a comfortable sleep environment with proper temperature and darkness.

Remember, sacrificing sleep for study time is like borrowing money at high interest. You might gain an hour of study time, but you lose two hours of productivity the next day. Good sleep habits are not a waste of time—they are an investment in your academic success.
questions.stem: What does the word "sacrificing" in Paragraph 1 probably mean?
options: A. Giving up something important to get something else.
B. Enjoying something pleasant with friends.
C. Studying hard without any rest.
D. Spending money on expensive things.
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文'sacrifice sleep to study'（为了学习而sacrifice睡眠），以及最后一段'sacrificing sleep for study time is like borrowing money at high interest'，可知sacrifice表示'牺牲、放弃'，即放弃睡眠来换取学习时间。选A。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Many students sacrifice sleep to study for exams, thinking that more study time means better grades. However, recent scientific research shows that this might actually hurt academic performance.

Scientists at a university in the US conducted a study with 500 high school students. They found that students who slept less than 6 hours a night had trouble concentrating in class. Their test scores were 20% lower than those who slept 8 hours or more.

"Sleep is like food for the brain," explains Dr. Sarah Chen, a sleep expert. "During sleep, your brain processes and stores information you learned during the day. Without enough sleep, your brain cannot do this job well."

The study also found an interesting rhythm in learning efficiency. Students who took short naps (15-30 minutes) after lunch performed better in afternoon classes. This is because the brain needs rest to work at its best.

However, many teenagers struggle to get enough sleep. School schedules, homework pressure, and the use of electronic devices before bed all contribute to this problem. The blue light from phones and computers can strain your eyes and make it harder to fall asleep (strain: 使疲劳；损伤).

To solve this issue, experts suggest three things. First, establish a regular sleep schedule and stick to it even on weekends. Second, avoid using electronic devices one hour before bedtime. Third, create a comfortable sleep environment with proper temperature and darkness.

Remember, sacrificing sleep for study time is like borrowing money at high interest. You might gain an hour of study time, but you lose two hours of productivity the next day. Good sleep habits are not a waste of time—they are an investment in your academic success.
questions.stem: What can we infer from the passage about naps?
options: A. All students must take naps to pass exams.
B. Short naps after lunch can improve learning efficiency.
C. Napping is a waste of valuable study time.
D. Students should sleep for at least one hour after lunch.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据第四段'Students who took short naps (15-30 minutes) after lunch performed better in afternoon classes'，可推断出短时间午睡能提高学习效率。A中must过于绝对；C与原文相反；D中at least one hour与原文15-30 minutes矛盾。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Many students sacrifice sleep to study for exams, thinking that more study time means better grades. However, recent scientific research shows that this might actually hurt academic performance.

Scientists at a university in the US conducted a study with 500 high school students. They found that students who slept less than 6 hours a night had trouble concentrating in class. Their test scores were 20% lower than those who slept 8 hours or more.

"Sleep is like food for the brain," explains Dr. Sarah Chen, a sleep expert. "During sleep, your brain processes and stores information you learned during the day. Without enough sleep, your brain cannot do this job well."

The study also found an interesting rhythm in learning efficiency. Students who took short naps (15-30 minutes) after lunch performed better in afternoon classes. This is because the brain needs rest to work at its best.

However, many teenagers struggle to get enough sleep. School schedules, homework pressure, and the use of electronic devices before bed all contribute to this problem. The blue light from phones and computers can strain your eyes and make it harder to fall asleep (strain: 使疲劳；损伤).

To solve this issue, experts suggest three things. First, establish a regular sleep schedule and stick to it even on weekends. Second, avoid using electronic devices one hour before bedtime. Third, create a comfortable sleep environment with proper temperature and darkness.

Remember, sacrificing sleep for study time is like borrowing money at high interest. You might gain an hour of study time, but you lose two hours of productivity the next day. Good sleep habits are not a waste of time—they are an investment in your academic success.
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. How to Pass Exams Easily
B. The Dangers of Electronic Devices
C. Sleep: The Secret to Better Grades
D. Why Students Should Study Less
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章开篇指出牺牲睡眠学习反而有害，接着用研究说明睡眠与成绩的关系，最后强调睡眠是学业成功的投资。C选项'Sleep: The Secret to Better Grades'（睡眠：取得好成绩的秘诀）最能概括全文。A过于宽泛；B只是文中一个细节；D与文章主旨不符，文章不是说少学习，而是强调睡眠的重要性。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Joining a school club is a great way to enrich your middle school life. Last term, I decided to join the Science Club. (17)______, I was nervous because I didn't know anyone there.

(18)______, the club leader, a friendly senior student, welcomed me warmly. She told me that the club was for everyone who loved science. Our first project was to build a small robot. (19)______, I didn't know how to start. The leader paired me with Tom, who patiently taught me the basics.

Step by step, I learned how to assemble the parts. (20)______, I not only made good friends but also won second prize in the school science competition. Joining the club was really a great experience.
questions.explanation: 根据后文'I was nervous'及下一段开头的转折，这里表示'起初'的感受。At first（起初，一开始）符合时间顺序和语境逻辑，与后文的转折形成对比。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Joining a school club is a great way to enrich your middle school life. Last term, I decided to join the Science Club. (17)______, I was nervous because I didn't know anyone there.

(18)______, the club leader, a friendly senior student, welcomed me warmly. She told me that the club was for everyone who loved science. Our first project was to build a small robot. (19)______, I didn't know how to start. The leader paired me with Tom, who patiently taught me the basics.

Step by step, I learned how to assemble the parts. (20)______, I not only made good friends but also won second prize in the school science competition. Joining the club was really a great experience.
questions.explanation: 前文说紧张害怕，后文说受到热烈欢迎，前后是转折关系。However（然而）表示转折，符合逻辑。注意However后常用逗号，与原文格式一致。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Joining a school club is a great way to enrich your middle school life. Last term, I decided to join the Science Club. (17)______, I was nervous because I didn't know anyone there.

(18)______, the club leader, a friendly senior student, welcomed me warmly. She told me that the club was for everyone who loved science. Our first project was to build a small robot. (19)______, I didn't know how to start. The leader paired me with Tom, who patiently taught me the basics.

Step by step, I learned how to assemble the parts. (20)______, I not only made good friends but also won second prize in the school science competition. Joining the club was really a great experience.
questions.explanation: 此处表示时间顺序：项目开始了，然后/接着我不知道如何开始。Then（然后，接着）表示时间上的承接。后文说老师给我配对搭档，也是按时间顺序发展的。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Joining a school club is a great way to enrich your middle school life. Last term, I decided to join the Science Club. (17)______, I was nervous because I didn't know anyone there.

(18)______, the club leader, a friendly senior student, welcomed me warmly. She told me that the club was for everyone who loved science. Our first project was to build a small robot. (19)______, I didn't know how to start. The leader paired me with Tom, who patiently taught me the basics.

Step by step, I learned how to assemble the parts. (20)______, I not only made good friends but also won second prize in the school science competition. Joining the club was really a great experience.
questions.explanation: 前文讲逐步学习组装，后文讲交到朋友并获奖，这是前文努力的结果。As a result（结果，因此）表示因果关系，总结前文带来的积极结果。
questions.label: 短文填空
